title: Self-Sustaining Life System

content.statement: The ultimate system runs without constant effort.
content.question: What one system, if made self-sustaining in 90 days, would change your whole life?
content.life_lens: Routines automatic; home clean without stress.
sop: List the systems currently held together by your memory and willpower alone.
Rate Automation, Alignment, and Consistency each 1-10 for your top system (see calculation, page 4).
Automate the piece that's currently 100% manual, bill pay, scheduling, restocking, reporting.
Remove one decision point that depends on willpower by making the default the right choice.
Refine monthly, a self-sustaining system still needs occasional maintenance, just not daily attention.
questions: What one system, if made self-sustaining in 90 days, would change your whole life?
What currently runs only because you personally remember to do it, and what happens the week you forget?
If you got sick for two weeks, which parts of your life would keep functioning without you?
content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10: Automation, Alignment, Consistency. Automation 7 times Alignment 8 times Consistency 6 equals 336. Higher means the system runs correctly even on weeks you're not paying attention; lower means it quietly stops the moment your attention shifts elsewhere.
content.closing_statement: Build systems that survive the weeks you forget them.

caption:
Principle Nº 189: Self-Sustaining Life System

The ultimate system runs without constant effort.

Ask yourself: What one system, if made self-sustaining in 90 days, would change your whole life?
